content: Most existing explorations of Self-Refine and LLM self-reflection mechanisms treat self-feedback as a single, monolithic perspective, but combining the two frameworks reveals that Self-Refine's iterative improvement loop can be powered by multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from modern self-reflection mechanisms to unlock stronger, training-free performance gains. This creates a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra supervised data or training, while addressing its common limitation of getting stuck in local feedback loops.
